templates.title: S2 Orientation — Assessment Foundations and Sim Requirements
templates.review_notes: Group 13 Week 1 Day 1 uses a dual parallel plan — two groups running concurrently (Plan A and Plan B). Confirm whether this split-group format is standard for S2 Week 1 or specific to Group 13. Plan B includes CoAEMSP Sim Requirements (Infant NAT Trauma, Geriatric Sepsis) — confirm Sim Req documentation process for first session.

stations.format_notes: Undifferentiated unconscious patient — baseline scenario.

stations.scenario_title: Cardiac Chest Pain — STEMI / Angina Suspect
stations.format_notes: Platinum documentation required. Plan A station.
S2 Platinum portal orientation and documentation workflow. Both plans.

stations.station_name: Skill Review — Student Request or Advanced Variation
stations.format_notes: Week 1 — orientation session. Skill review is light intro to format only.

templates.title: MOI, Med Draw Drills, Rapid Trauma Assessment, Respiratory

stations.format_notes: SNHD context — accurate medication draws in timed format.
Detailed head-to-toe assessment on manikin with moulage. Half students step out while other half runs; moulage reset then groups swap. Faculty Guide referenced.
152 Wk 1 Cardiac A&P. 192 Wk 1 Trauma overview running concurrently.

stations.station_name: Skill Review — Student Request or Advanced Variation
stations.format_notes: Week 1 — keep at refresher level, no pressure.

templates.title: Shock, Hemorrhage, and Respiratory — Week 2

stations.format_notes: Current week content (192 Wk2 — Shock & Bleeding). Penetrating chest trauma with hemorrhagic shock. Tourniquet not applicable — pressure dressing, IV access, fluid resuscitation, tension PTX consideration. Platinum documentation required.

stations.scenario_title: Extremity Bleed with Hemorrhagic Shock — Tourniquet and Resuscitation
stations.format_notes: Current week content (192 Wk2 — Shock & Bleeding). Life-threatening extremity hemorrhage — tourniquet, wound packing, IV access, fluid resuscitation. Straightforward hemorrhage control appropriate for early S2.

stations.scenario_title: Respiratory Distress — COPD Exacerbation with Hypercapnic Failure
stations.format_notes: Previous content rotation (Week 1 respiratory arc). COPD exacerbation progressing toward hypercapnic failure. CPAP/BiPAP decision, bronchodilators, positioning. Standard format: 2 stations current week content, 1 station previous content.

stations.station_name: Skill Review — Student Request or Advanced Variation
stations.format_notes: Poll students before lab. If requests: focused refresher. If no requests: instructor-selected variation. Week 2 — keep at refresher level.

templates.title: Cardiac Dysrhythmia Scenarios and ACLS Drill Cards — S2 Week 2
templates.review_notes: Content derived from Group 10 Week 2 cardiac component only — sheep pluck removed (not ready Week 2, scheduling dependent on Dr. Kat and specimen availability). Confirm Group 13 Week 2 Day 2 content with lead instructor.

stations.scenario_title: Cardiac Dysrhythmia — Tachycardia with Pulse (ACLS Algorithm)
stations.format_notes: ACLS algorithm introduction — tachy with pulse. Two groups: one runs scenario with monitor actions, other observes and critiques, then debrief and switch. Multiple scenarios available. See ACLS scenarios on instructor desk. Instructor: J. Lomonaco.

stations.scenario_title: Cardiac Dysrhythmia — Bradycardia with Pulse (ACLS Algorithm)
stations.format_notes: ACLS algorithm introduction — brady with pulse. Paired with tachy station — groups rotate. Observation and critique format. Builds toward ACLS certification Week 6.
Introduction to ACLS drill card format used in Weeks 3-5 prep arc. Students familiarize with algorithm decision points. Foundation for Week 3 Day 1 formal drill card sessions.

stations.station_name: Skill Review — Student Request or Advanced Variation
stations.format_notes:
DAILY SKILL REVIEW — runs every regular S2 lab session.
Format:
  • Poll students 24-48 hours before lab (via Cowork or group message) for skill review requests.
  • If requests received: run as focused refresher station. No evaluation — practice and feedback only.
  • If no requests received by lab morning: instructor runs an advanced variation from the list below.

Advanced variation options (escalate progressively as semester advances):
  Early S2 (Weeks 1-5): Timed skill drill — ETI in 60 sec, IO access first attempt, IV with one hand
  Mid S2 (Weeks 6-10): Difficult scenario variation — failed airway, non-standard anatomy, combative patient
  Late S2 (Weeks 11-15): S3-style drill card format — 8-min timed station, student runs solo, instructor evaluates

Instructor notes:
  • Track what gets requested — pattern of requests = cohort comfort gaps.
  • Track what no-requests weeks required — if advanced versions run every week, cohort is ready for S3 format.
  • No Platinum documentation required for this station.
  • Assign 1 instructor. Station runs concurrent with other stations.

stations.station_name: ACLS Drill Cards — Tachy and Brady
stations.format_notes: Drill Card 1: Tachycardia algorithm. Drill Card 2: Bradycardia algorithm. Run both cards at same station, students rotate through. Builds toward ACLS certification Week 6.

stations.station_name: Skill Review — Student Request or Advanced Variation
stations.format_notes: Week 3 — ACLS prep arc. Advanced variation: timed drill card under 3 min.

templates.title: Multi-Algorithm Arrests and Trauma — ACLS Prep Arc

stations.format_notes: ACLS prep arc — Week 3 of 5-week build toward Week 6 ACLS certification. Multi-algorithm arrest requiring algorithm transitions. Moved from original Wk4D2 — displaced by burn center field trip.
ACLS prep — full algorithm sequence including asystole management and ROSC debriefing.

stations.scenario_title: Trauma — Branching Path: Life-Threatening Extremity Bleed
stations.format_notes: Branching trauma scenario. Life-threatening hemorrhage with shock management. TXA, fluid resuscitation, tourniquet decision-making.

stations.format_notes: Equipment: Megacode Kelly + SimPad Plus + RT Laptop as monitor.

templates.title: UMC Burn Center Field Trip

stations.format_notes: External clinical field experience at UMC Burn Center. Scheduled June 5. Replaces regular lab session for this date. Students observe burn wound care, surgical debridement, and long-term burn rehabilitation. Counts toward clinical exposure hours. No scenario stations — attendance and reflection documentation required. Coordinate transport and arrival time with burn center contact in advance.

templates.title: Megacodes and Trauma — Final ACLS Prep

stations.scenario_title: Megacode — Afib RVR → VF Arrest → PEA → ROSC
stations.format_notes: Full megacode with ROSC endpoint. Final pre-ACLS prep. 152 Wk 5 Clinical MI + Shock (cardiogenic). SHOCK STAGE 1: both hemorrhagic (192 Wk4) and cardiogenic (152 Wk5) angles now active. Instructor: B. Hartnell.
Megacode Kelly — hospital monitor + computer. Instructor: M. Schaffer.

stations.scenario_title: Auto vs. Pedestrian with Spinal Injury and Distracting Hemorrhage
stations.format_notes: 192 Wk 5 Spine and Nervous System. Instructor: B. Young.

templates.title: Sheep Pluck Anatomy Lab — Flexible Scheduling
templates.review_notes: FLEXIBLE SCHEDULING: Sheep pluck placement varies each semester based on Dr. Kat ER schedule availability and specimen ordering lead time. Currently placeholder at Week 5 Day 2. May shift to any available session in Weeks 3-8. Coordinate with Dr. Kat and confirm specimen order 2-3 weeks in advance. If sheep pluck is not available this date, run ACLS prep stations instead (see format_notes).

stations.format_notes: SCHEDULING DEPENDENT — confirm date with Dr. Kat before locking. Lung dissection: overview of lung anatomy, inflation demonstration, PEEP demonstration, tissue dissection. Equipment: sheep pluck lung dissection worksheet, one pluck per two students. Instructor: B. Hartnell (or Dr. Kat). Two-group rotation with heart station — students stay at table, instructors rotate halfway.

SCHEDULING DEPENDENT — confirm date with Dr. Kat before locking. Cardiac dissection: overview and dissection of heart anatomy. Equipment: Heart Dissection.pdf. Instructor: R. Young (or Dr. Kat). Groups rotate from lungs station halfway through.

stations.station_name: ACLS Prep — Fallback If Sheep Pluck Unavailable
stations.format_notes: FALLBACK CONTENT if sheep pluck not available this date. Run final ACLS prep megacode stations in preparation for Week 6 certification. Scenario library from Week 5 Day 1.

stations.scenario_title: Auto-Pedestrian FBAO — Hidden Pit, Hidden Trauma
stations.format_notes: Complex scenario: FBAO with concealed traumatic injuries from pedestrian strike. Requires Magill forceps for ALS FBAO removal plus trauma management. Megacode Kelly. Instructor: B. Hartnell, S. Peterson. Lifepack monitor, Simpad controller. Platinum skills: FBAO Magill, needle decompression available.

stations.scenario_title: Autonomic Dysreflexia — Missed Clues Lead to Crisis
stations.format_notes: Spinal cord injury patient in autonomic dysreflexia — hypertensive emergency with concealed trigger. Students must identify and eliminate stimulus. 2007 medium-fidelity manikin. Instructor: J. Lomonaco. Lifepack 2, non-networked controller.

stations.scenario_title: Pediatric Drowning — 3-Year-Old Found Unresponsive in Pool
stations.format_notes: Pediatric submersion — resuscitation, hypothermia management, family dynamics. Sim Baby (RT manikin, toddler-sized). Instructors: B. Hartnell, S. Peterson. Lifepack 3, RT Laptop controller. SimNet required. Peds trauma scenario.
